editorialSummary.text: Cozy, colorful kitchen presenting contemporary takes on classic Pakistani and Indian dishes.

text.text: The service started off so so with a guy that didn’t acknowledge us for a while until a younger guy came out and helped us. The inside of the restaurant is very nice and updated! The food was nothing too special there wasn’t enough species and tasted more creamy and sweet. The waiter was super friendly and checked on us multiple times. I found the prices to be a bit pricey compared to other businesses that offer the same thing. I would definitely recommend at least giving the restaurant a try if you’re in the area.

text.text:
It had been a while since my last visit, and I was pleasantly surprised by the revamped outdoor patio. The setup with heat lamps and shaded seating makes it feel cozy and upscale, especially in the evening when the place is bustling. They also have a large indoor space, so capacity is not an issue.

We tried the dahi puri from the appetizers, and it really stood out. The flavors were well balanced, and it is easily one of the better versions I have had compared to other Indian spots. The chicken samosa was solid, and the crispy chicken zinger burger came with a generous portion of fried chicken. We also got the chicken boti thali with garlic naan, which was flavorful and paired well with the dal. Everything went nicely with chai on the side.

The service was friendly and added to the overall experience. It is great to see the updates to this location, and it definitely makes me want to come back more often.

text.text:
Delicious flavor!
We went to this modern Asian restaurant for dinner with our friends. The ambiance was warm and welcoming. The staff was attentive and friendly. The food serving time was not excessively long. We had the following food-
Appetizer-Chicken Memoni Samosas, Aloo Tikki
Main Course-Chicken Boti Sizzler Thali, Lamb Gosht Thali, Shami Kabab Thali (replacing rice with naan)
The food was fresh and flavorful, and it had a fair portion size for sharing. I would suggest going here.

text.text: This is a big branch of the most popular Pakistani restaurant in the Bay Area. This is a nice branch with plenty of indoor and outdoor seating. The restaurant gets very busy on weekends for all times including brunch, lunch, evening, and dinner. The menu is vast with plenty of options for snacks and meals. There are both meat based as well as vegetarian options, which are smartly written in green ink. The food tastes really authentic, the portion sizes are super generous, and the prices are relative to the quality and quantity. The staff are very nice, despite being really busy they make sure to attend to you. Food is also delivered pretty fast. Seating is only assigned by the staff and even at busier hours they do it quickly. Highly recommended for good quality Halal Pakistani food.

text.text:
I’ve been reviewing restaurants for a long time, and Zareen’s in Palo Alto is one of those places that continues to live up to its reputation.

From the moment we sat down, the place felt lively and welcoming. It was busy, but comfortable, with a good flow and a positive energy that immediately sets expectations high.

We ordered the chapli kabab, chicken tikka masala, chicken sizzler, garlic naan, regular naan, and mango and rose falooda. The chapli kabab was juicy, well-spiced, and full of flavor, cooked just right without being greasy. The chicken tikka masala was rich and balanced, with tender chicken and a sauce that wasn’t overly creamy or sweet. The chicken sizzler arrived hot and sizzling, packed with smoky flavor and generous portions.

Both the garlic naan and regular naan were fresh, soft, and nicely cooked, with good texture and flavor. They paired well with the curries and rounded out the meal exactly how you’d want.

We finished with the mango and rose falooda, which was refreshing and aromatic without being overly sweet—a perfect way to end the meal.

Service was smooth and efficient throughout, but can improve. The staff was polite, attentive when needed, and handled the crowd well without making the experience feel rushed.

The atmosphere brings everything together. It’s clean, vibrant, and comfortable, making it easy to enjoy your meal whether you’re dining with family, friends, or just stopping in for a reliable, high-quality meal.

Zareen’s Palo Alto delivers where it matters most: consistently great food, a welcoming environment, and dependable service. It’s a place I return to confidently and recommend without hesitation.
